title: Eragrostis brownii
taxonomy: Eragrostis brownii is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. The genus Eragrostis is a group of grasses that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Eragrostis brownii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a group of grasses characterized by their two-flowered spikelets. The species is also classified as part of the order Poales, which includes various families of flowering plants that produce one-celled anthers. This classification is based on the species' morphological and anatomical characteristics.
uses: Eragrostis brownii is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its extracts have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
